title: Java Output
content:
### Printing to Console

Java provides several ways to display output to the console using the `System` class.

---

### Output Methods

| Method | Behavior |
|--------|----------|
| `System.out.println()` | Prints text and moves to next line |
| `System.out.print()` | Prints text without moving to next line |
| `System.out.printf()` | Prints formatted text |

---

### Escape Sequences

| Sequence | Result |
|----------|--------|
| `\n` | New line |
| `\t` | Tab |
| `\\` | Backslash |
| `\"` | Double quote |

---

### Formatting with printf

```java
System.out.printf("Hello %s, you are %d years old", "John", 25);
```

| Specifier | Type |
|-----------|------|
| %d | Integer |
| %f | Floating point |
| %s | String |
| %c | Character |
| %b | Boolean |

> **Key Point:** `println` adds a newline at the end, while `print` does not. Use `printf` for precise formatting control.

quiz.question: Which method prints text without moving to a new line?
Which escape sequence adds a new line?
